White Knuckle How to Mod the Game \[outdated?\]

By Qwergin

This guide shows you how to create and install mods on White Knuckle. I would like to clarify that there are more resources on the White Knuckle discord server for modding. But, I still made this guide to catch people's attention on modding directly in a Steam guide.

Created by

 Qwergin  
 Offline 

Category: Characters, Game Modes, Gameplay Basics, Loot, Maps or Levels, Modding or Configuration, Walkthroughs

Languages: English

Posted 

Updated 

Apr 30, 2025 @ 5:13pm

Dec 19, 2025 @ 11:46am

| 5,613 | Unique Visitors   |
| ----- | ----------------- |
| 47    | Current Favorites |

Guide Index

Overview 

Introduction 

Installing What You Need 

Installing Mods 

Creating Mods 

Installing Mods with Resourceful Hands 

Creating Mods with Resourceful Hands 

Comments 

 Introduction 

 Hello! 

I made this guide to teach people how to create mods, and add other peoples mods to White Knuckle to personalize their experience better.  White Knuckle is on Thunderstore and r2modman, but some people publish their mods as just files you have to put into the game. There are a lot of modding resources on the discord server, which can be found  at this link. \[discord.gg\]  
  
You can find my other White Knuckle guides here:  
 White Knuckle 100% All Achievements Guide   
 White Knuckle How to Enter the Developer Playground   
 White Knuckle All Items Detailed Guide   
 White Knuckle All Creatures and Hazards Detailed Guide   
 White Knuckle How to Use the Cheat Console   
 White Knuckle All Perks   
 White Knuckle All Logbook Unlocks   
White Knuckle How to Get Good at the Game  
White Knuckle Abbreviations and Terms  
White Knuckle Best Routes For Every Room (W.I.P) 

 Installing What You Need 

 To mod White Knuckle, you are going to need BepInEx. Go to  BepInEx on Github, \[github.com\] and download a x64 version.  
Once you've downloaded the file, extract it.  
Then, go to steam, go to White Knuckle, click the settings icon, click manage, and then click browse local files. This will open the file folder for White Knuckle.  
  
  
You must drag all of the contents of your zipped file straight into the White Knuckle file.  
After you do this, run White Knuckle, and then close it.  
After you run and close White Knuckle, it generates the files you need.  
  
You've prepared yourself to add mods! Read the other sections to learn how to actually add the mods. 

 Installing Mods 

_When using mods, I recommend enabling cheats so leaderboard stats are not saved if the mod does not already disable leaderboard stats. If you are caught cheating with mods you might be punished or unable to have leaderboard scores again or something._  
  
The best place to find mods is the discord server. They have a forum where you can find mods people have made. However, **BE CAREFUL!!!** You should verify whether a mod file is safe or not.  
  
Anyways, when you download a file, you have to bring it to the "Plugins" folder in BepInEx. Then it should work as intended.  
  
An example mod I will be using for this guide is a Zero Gravity mod from the discord server.  
I download the .dll file, and then I drag it to the plugins folder in BepInEx.  
After I do this, I run the game, and the mod works!  
  
 **If I want to disable the mod, I would close the game and move the .dll file anywhere that isn't the BepInEx plugins folder.**   
  
It's really that simple for the simple mods like these.  
  
However, many mods require you to do more steps. These instructions are usually in the mod download area itself. 

 Creating Mods 

 This section of the guide contains links to resources that teach you how to mod White Knuckle.  
GO TO THE DISCORD SERVER FOR MORE INFORMATION!!!\[discord.gg\]  
These are simply just general snippets of information that can help you get started.  
  
  
**Basics of WK sprite assets modding, made by @Maxifyz on discord.\[docs.google.com\]**  
  
  
**YouTube Unity sound modding tutorial.**  
  
  
**Level Editing Unity Tutorial by @Atom on discord.\[docs.google.com\]**  
  
  
**Some information about sound modding I found.**  

 Installing Mods with Resourceful Hands 

 Resourceful Hands is a very convenient new mod that has been released. It makes replacing textures and sounds incredibly easy. Here is how to install mods with Resourceful Hands. It is more organized because this is a bit more complex, but Resourceful Hands is convenient and easy to use. It is also work in progress though, so it might be buggy sometimes.  
  
**You can install it with Thunderstore/r2modman btw, below is the manual installation which is much harder to do**  
* You need BepInEx installed first. You can find out how to install that in previous sections.
* Install the Resourceful Hands file on the discord server in the wk-mod-releases forum channel. It is currently version v0.9.61.
* After you install it, unzip it and put the entire unzipped folder in the "plugins" folder in the BepInEx folder.
* I think you have to open the game and close it for this to happen, but there should be an RHPacks folder in the config folder in the BepInEx folder.
* Download any mod for Resourceful Hands in the discord server. For an example, we'll use a mod that I made that replaces all announcer clips with meme clips.
* Download the coolerannouncer mod folder, and then unzip it. Drag the entire coolerannouncer unzipped folder into the RHPacks folder.  
**Below is what you do after either installing a mod manually or from Thunderstore**  
Open the game, and go to settings. You will see "packs" is there. You can adjust the packs however you want from this screen.  
    
    
_OLD VERSION:_
* Open the game, and open the cheat console.
* Type "listpacks" to make sure you see the pack there.
* Type "enablepack" and then the GUID or index number after enablepack. The GUID for my mod is "coolerannouncer" so to enable it I would type "enablepack coolerannouncer"
  
Screenshots:  
**The Resourceful Hands mod. (OLD VERSION IS SHOWN IN THE SCREENSHOT)**   
  
**What your folders should look like.**  
  
**Commands typed into the cheat console.**  

 Creating Mods with Resourceful Hands

 Yes, most of the info was for other players to let them know, i linked your guide in my comment too.  
  
To clear it up, i was talking about the disabled stats, achievements and leader-boards, i tested that and it does not last. You are correct that you do not need to type "cheats" again each time in order to use the cheat commands. It will remember that, but it will disable the cheat mode until you use another command. The cheat mode itself is only active, when you see the red text in the top right.  
  
That is important, in case you run a mod which for example makes the game significantly easier and you don't want to ruin the public leader-boards, your stats and achievements.  
  
There was already someone who accidentally got in the top 10 with a mod, i gave him the advise to turn on cheat mode to circumvent that. The info has to be reliable though, in order to not make the mistake of someone accidentally hitting first place on the leader-boards with a jet-pack mod or something.

input.name: Spike Balance Mod
input.overview: Build a BepInEx 5 Harmony plugin for White Knuckle that procedurally places damaging spikes on upward-facing surfaces, adds a health bar UI, and rebalances stamina/health recovery based on whether the player is resting on spike-covered ground versus climbing in the air.
input.plan:
# Spike Balance Mod — Implementation Plan

## Goal

A BepInEx plugin that:

1. **Spikes** — After each level/scene load, place spike hazards on colliders whose upward-facing area exceeds a configurable normal threshold (`normal.y >= minUpDot`, covering floors, ledges, and steep ramps per your choice).
2. **Damage** — Contact with spike surfaces damages the player over time (or on tick) while they are “on spike ground.”
3. **Health bar** — On-screen bar showing current/max health (game may hide this by default).
4. **Resource trade-off**
   - **On spike ground:** stamina recovers; health drains from spikes.
   - **Off spike ground:** stamina uses vanilla behavior; health regenerates after a short configurable delay.

References: [Steam modding guide](https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3473004632) (BepInEx x64, drop DLL in `BepInEx/plugins`), community pattern from [white_knuckle_control_goo](https://github.com/tonebacas/white_knuckle_control_goo) and [WKMultiMod](https://github.com/Shen-X-L/WKMultiMod) (Harmony + `Assembly-CSharp.dll`, `netstandard2.1`), UI precedent [Stamina Display on Thunderstore](https://thunderstore.io/c/white-knuckle/p/overmet15/Stamina_Display/).

---

## Current workspace state

Your folder [`c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle`](c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle) is a **partial game data tree** only:

| Present | Missing (required for coding) |
|---------|-------------------------------|
| `White Knuckle_Data\` (cosmetic JSON mods, `link.xml`, `ScriptingAssemblies.json`) | `White Knuckle.exe` |
| MonoBleedingEdge | `White Knuckle_Data\Managed\Assembly-CSharp.dll` |
| | `BepInEx\` |

Useful type hints from [`link.xml`](White Knuckle_Data/StreamingAssets/aa/AddressablesLink/link.xml): `ENT_Player`, `ENT_Player/Hand`, `App_SolarKnight_Player`, `Damageable/DamageEffect`, `UT_DamagePlayer`, `CL_Handhold_Damage`, `HandholdManager`, collider types.

**Phase 0** is unblocking development in this folder (you selected this as the install target).

---

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph boot [BepInEx Boot]
    Plugin[SpikeBalancePlugin]
  end

  subgraph load [Level Load]
    SceneHook[Harmony: scene/level loaded]
    Scanner[SpikeSurfaceScanner]
    Placer[SpikePlacer]
    SceneHook --> Scanner --> Placer
  end

  subgraph runtime [Each Frame / FixedUpdate]
    GroundDetect[SpikeGroundDetector]
    Resources[PlayerResourceController]
    UI[HealthBarUI]
    GroundDetect --> Resources
    Resources --> UI
  end

  Plugin --> SceneHook
  Plugin --> GroundDetect
  Placer -->|marks colliders| SpikeLayer[SpikeSurfaceMarker]
  GroundDetect --> SpikeLayer
```

---

## Phase 0 — Complete install and mod toolchain

1. Copy or install the **full Steam build** into this workspace so you have:
   - `White Knuckle.exe`
   - `White Knuckle_Data\Managed\*.dll`
2. Install **BepInEx 5 x64** per the [Steam guide](https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3473004632): extract into game root, run game once, confirm `BepInEx\plugins` and logs exist.
3. Set `HideManagerGameObject = true` in `BepInEx\config\BepInEx.cfg` (recommended by existing WK mods).
4. For leaderboard safety: enable in-game **cheat mode** during testing so stats/leaderboards are not affected (per guide + author comments).

---

## Phase 1 — Mod project scaffold (sibling to game root)

Create a **separate source folder** (keeps game files clean), e.g.:

`c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle Mod\` (or `White Knuckle\SpikeBalanceMod-src\`)

| Piece | Choice |
|-------|--------|
| Template | `dotnet new bepinex5plugin` — TFM `netstandard2.1`, Unity **2022.3.x** (match your `settings.json` `StandaloneWindows64`) |
| References | Copy-on-build from [`White Knuckle_Data\Managed`](White Knuckle_Data) — at minimum `Assembly-CSharp.dll`, `UnityEngine.CoreModule.dll`, `UnityEngine.PhysicsModule.dll`, `UnityEngine.UI.dll`, `DarkMachineUI.dll` if UI hooks need it |
| Output | Post-build copy `SpikeBalanceMod.dll` → `[GameRoot]\BepInEx\plugins\` |
| Deps | BepInEx 5 + Harmony 2.x (bundled with template) |

**Do not commit** `Managed\` DLLs to git; use `.gitignore` + optional `GAME_ROOT` MSBuild property pointing at `..\White Knuckle`.

---

## Phase 2 — API discovery (dnSpy / ILSpy)

Before writing gameplay logic, inspect `Assembly-CSharp.dll` and document (in a short `API_NOTES.md` in the mod repo):

| Question | Likely types to inspect |
|----------|-------------------------|
| Player singleton / instance | `ENT_Player`, `App_SolarKnight_Player`, `UT_GetPlayer` |
| Health pool + damage | `Damageable`, `UT_DamagePlayer`, `UT_Damage`, `Damageable/DamageEffect` |
| Stamina read/write | `ENT_Player/Hand`, `HandItem`, stamina-related fields on player or hand manager |
| “On ground” vs climbing | Player state enum, ground checks, foot/body colliders, or contact with non-handhold geometry |
| Level load hook | Scene manager, run/session start, `HandholdManager` init, procedural room spawn events |
| Existing spike/hazard prefab | Search assets for spike, nail, hazard, `CL_Handhold_Damage` — **prefer reusing** a game prefab via `Resources` / Addressables / instantiated template for correct VFX and damage integration |

**Decision gate:** If the game already exposes health on `Damageable`, **read/write that** instead of a parallel custom health float (avoids desync with death/vanilla damage). If health is opaque, add a mod-side overlay health synced via Harmony on damage events.

---

## Phase 3 — Spike placement (`SpikeSurfaceScanner` + `SpikePlacer`)

**Trigger:** Harmony postfix on the method that finishes loading a playable room/level (exact target from Phase 2 — e.g. scene loaded, `HandholdManager` ready, or run start).

**Scan algorithm (v1):**

1. Collect candidate colliders: `MeshCollider`, `BoxCollider`, `TerrainCollider` in the active scene (exclude player, hands, triggers, UI, existing hazards via layer/tag denylist from config).
2. For each collider:
   - **MeshCollider:** sample triangles (or grid on bounds top face); keep samples where `normal.y >= minUpDot` (default ~0.7, configurable for “all up-facing” including ramps).
   - **Primitive colliders:** treat top face / upward faces using `transform.up` and bounds.
3. **Dedupe** spike positions with a minimum spacing grid (e.g. 0.5–1.0 m) so large floors are not thousands of spikes.
4. **Filter exclusions:** handhold-only surfaces, ropes, breakables, NPC volumes, spawn zones (discovered via layers/tags during testing).

**Place spikes:**

- Instantiate cloned hazard prefab (from Phase 2) **or** spawn a simple prefab: mesh + `SpikeSurfaceMarker` + trigger/collision child aligned to surface normal (`SeppePeelman.EditorTools.SurfaceAlignTool` in assemblies suggests the game cares about surface alignment — match that orientation).
- Parent spikes under a single `SpikeBalanceMod_Root` empty for easy cleanup on reload.
- Add component `SpikeSurfaceMarker` (mod-owned) on spike root or enlarged trigger slightly above the surface.

**Config keys (BepInEx):** `minUpDot`, `spikeSpacing`, `maxSpikesPerRoom`, `enableSpikes`, `spikeDamagePerSecond`.

**Performance:** Cap spikes per room; optional async spread across frames if scan stutters.

---

## Phase 4 — Ground detection (`SpikeGroundDetector`)

White Knuckle is climb-focused; “on spike ground” must be defined explicitly:

**Recommended v1 definition:**

- Player is **on spike ground** when:
  - A short downward cast (or capsule overlap) from the player body/feet hits a collider with `SpikeSurfaceMarker`, **and**
  - Player is **not** only supported by handholds (optional: require no active hand grip, or below a vertical velocity threshold, from Phase 2 state).

Tune with config: `groundCheckDistance`, `requireNoHandGrip`.

Expose `bool IsOnSpikeGround` updated in `FixedUpdate` for stable physics.

---

## Phase 5 — Resource rules (`PlayerResourceController` + Harmony patches)

Implement as a small state machine driven by `IsOnSpikeGround`:

| State | Stamina | Health |
|-------|---------|--------|
| On spike ground | Add `staminaRegenPerSec` (patch or override vanilla drain) | Subtract `spikeDamagePerSec` (reuse `UT_DamagePlayer` if safe, else direct `Damageable` API) |
| Off spike ground | Vanilla stamina | After `healthRegenDelay` seconds since leaving spike ground, add `healthRegenPerSec` up to max |

**Harmony targets (fill in after Phase 2):**

- Postfix on stamina drain/update — boost regen when `IsOnSpikeGround`.
- Prefix/postfix on health damage — optionally reduce non-spike damage or leave vanilla; ensure spike damage is classified so it does not double-apply.
- Optional: suppress vanilla health regen while on spikes if the game already regenerates health.

**Edge cases:**

- Death at 0 health — defer to vanilla death flow.
- Pause / menu — freeze timers.
- Room transition — reset regen delay timer and clear cached ground state.

---

## Phase 6 — Health bar UI (`HealthBarUI`)

Pattern similar to [Stamina Display](https://thunderstore.io/c/white-knuckle/p/overmet15/Stamina_Display/):

1. Create a `Canvas` (screen space overlay) in `Awake` or first run frame — `DontDestroyOnLoad` optional for persistence across scenes.
2. `Image` fill bar + background; read `currentHealth / maxHealth` from discovered API each `Update`.
3. Config: position, size, colors, show/hide, fade when full.
4. If vanilla already shows health in some modes, add config `forceShowHealthBar` and only enable when hidden.

Avoid conflicting with `DarkMachineUI` — test HUD layering (sort order).

---

## Phase 7 — Plugin entry, config, logging

**`SpikeBalancePlugin.cs`:**

- `[BepInPlugin(GUID, Name, Version)]`
- `Awake`: bind all `ConfigEntry` values, apply Harmony patches, subscribe to scene events.
- `OnDestroy` / disable: unpatch, destroy spike root.

**Logging:** Log spike count per room, ground state transitions (debug flag), patch failures.

**Example config section:**

```ini
[Spikes]
Enabled = true
MinUpDot = 0.7
Spacing = 0.75
DamagePerSecond = 5

[Resources]
StaminaRegenPerSecOnSpikes = 8
HealthRegenDelay = 1.5
HealthRegenPerSec = 3

[UI]
ShowHealthBar = true
```

---

## Phase 8 — Testing plan

| Test | Pass criteria |
|------|----------------|
| BepInEx loads | Log shows plugin version; no patch exceptions |
| Small room | Spikes appear on upward surfaces; not on walls/ceilings |
| Stand on spikes | Health drops; stamina rises |
| Climb / jump off | Spike damage stops; after delay, health rises |
| Stamina exhaustion while on spikes | Can recover stamina at cost of health — intentional balance |
| Re-enter room | Old spikes cleaned; new scan runs |
| Performance | Stable FPS with `maxSpikesPerRoom` cap |
| Compatibility | No crash with other BepInEx plugins; document conflict risk |

Test in **Developer Playground** first if available (mentioned in the Steam guide ecosystem), then a normal run.

---

## Suggested file layout

```
White Knuckle Mod/
  SpikeBalanceMod.csproj
  Plugin/SpikeBalancePlugin.cs
  Spikes/SpikeSurfaceScanner.cs
  Spikes/SpikePlacer.cs
  Spikes/SpikeSurfaceMarker.cs
  Player/SpikeGroundDetector.cs
  Player/PlayerResourceController.cs
  Patches/StaminaPatches.cs
  Patches/HealthPatches.cs
  Patches/SceneLoadPatches.cs
  UI/HealthBarUI.cs
  API_NOTES.md          # filled during Phase 2
  README.md             # install + config + cheat mode warning
```

Game folder after build:

```
White Knuckle/
  BepInEx/plugins/SpikeBalanceMod.dll
```

---

## Risks and mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Game update breaks Harmony signatures | Pin game version in README; re-run dnSpy after updates |
| No true “ground” in climbing | Explicit spike-ground raycast + optional “no hand grip” rule |
| Procedural rooms spawn after load | Hook later event or retry scan with delay (config `scanDelaySeconds`) |
| Too many spikes | Spacing grid + hard cap |
| Leaderboard integrity | Enable cheat mode during modded play (per guide) |
| Obfuscated/private fields | Use `[HarmonyPatch]` with `AccessTools.FieldRef` or public APIs only |

---

## Implementation order (milestones)

1. **M0:** Full game + BepInEx in workspace; empty plugin logs on boot.
2. **M1:** Phase 2 API notes; health bar reads real values (even before spikes).
3. **M2:** Spike placement in one test room; visual-only spikes.
4. **M3:** Spike damage + ground detection.
5. **M4:** Stamina-on-spikes / health-off-spikes with delay.
6. **M5:** Config polish, performance cap, README.

---

## Out of scope for v1 (optional later)

- Thunderstore packaging / r2modman manifest
- Resourceful Hands texture packs (cosmetic-only path in guide)
- Networking (WKMultiMod-style)
- Per-room spike presets or manual spike maps

todos.content: Complete game install in workspace (exe + Managed DLLs) and install BepInEx 5 x64; run once to generate folders
Create BepInEx netstandard2.1 plugin project with GAME_ROOT DLL copy build and deploy to BepInEx/plugins
Decompile Assembly-CSharp.dll; document player, Damageable, stamina, ground state, level-load hooks, hazard prefabs in API_NOTES.md
Implement HealthBarUI reading real health values (M1 milestone)
Implement SpikeSurfaceScanner + SpikePlacer with minUpDot, spacing, cap; hook level load
Implement SpikeGroundDetector + spike DPS while on spike ground
Harmony patches for stamina regen on spikes and delayed health regen off spikes
Test in playground + full run; add config, logging, README with cheat-mode warning

content.text:
<user_query>

# White Knuckle API Notes (Spike Balance Mod)

Discovered via Mono.Cecil on `Assembly-CSharp.dll` (Unity 2022.3.62).

## Player

| Type | Notes |
|------|--------|
| `ENT_Player` | Main player; base type `GameEntity` |
| `ENT_Player.Hand` | Nested hand state; `gripStrength` (float) is hand stamina/grip |
| `ENT_Player.hands` | `Hand[]` field |
| `ENT_Player.isGrounded` | bool field |
| `ENT_Player.IsGrounded(bool)` | Ground check method |
| `ENT_Player.HandStamina(int, Hand, bool, bool, float)` | Vanilla grip/stamina update |
| `ENT_Player.Damage(DamageInfo)` | Apply damage |
| `ENT_Player.infiniteStamina` | bool cheat flag |

## Health

| Type | Field / method |
|------|----------------|
| `GameEntity` | `health`, `maxHealth` (float fields) |
| `Damageable.DamageInfo` | Nested struct/class: `amount`, `type`, `tags`, `position`, `force`, ... |
| `Damageable.Damage(float)` | Simple damage API on some entities |

## Hazards reference

| Type | Notes |
|------|--------|
| `CL_Handhold_Damage` | Handhold that damages; `damage`, `damageRate`, `handStrainDamage` |
| `UT_DamagePlayer` | `DamagePlayer(float)` utility |

No dedicated `Spike` gameplay type in assembly; mod uses procedural spike objects.

## Level load

| Type | Method |
|------|--------|
| `CL_GameManager` | `LoadScene`, `LoadLevels`, `LoadIn` |

Mod hooks `UnityEngine.SceneManagement.SceneManager.sceneLoaded` plus delayed coroutine retry for procedural rooms.

## Runtime access

- Player: `Object.FindObjectOfType<ENT_Player>()`
- Health fields: `AccessTools.Field(typeof(GameEntity), "health")` (fields may be non-public)
- Grip: `AccessTools.Field(typeof(ENT_Player).GetNestedType("Hand"), "gripStrength")`

input.contents:
# Spike Balance Mod

BepInEx plugin for **White Knuckle** that covers upward-facing surfaces with damaging spikes, shows a health bar, and rebalances resources:

- **On spike ground:** hand grip (stamina) recovers, health drains.
- **Off spike ground:** vanilla stamina behavior; health regenerates after a short delay.

## Requirements

- White Knuckle (Steam) with **BepInEx 5 x64** installed in the game folder
- `HideManagerGameObject = true` in `BepInEx/config/BepInEx.cfg` (recommended)

## Install

1. Build the mod (from this folder):

   ```bash
   dotnet build -c Release
   ```

   The DLL is copied automatically to `../../White Knuckle/BepInEx/plugins/SpikeBalanceMod.dll`.

2. Or copy `bin/Release/SpikeBalanceMod.dll` manually into `BepInEx/plugins/`.

3. Launch the game. Check `BepInEx/LogOutput.log` for `Spike Balance Mod loaded`.

## Leaderboards / stats

Enable in-game **cheat mode** while using mods so runs do not affect leaderboards or achievements. See the [Steam modding guide](https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3473004632).

## Configuration

After first run, edit `BepInEx/config/com.spikebalance.whiteknuckle.cfg`:

| Section | Key | Default | Description |
|---------|-----|---------|-------------|
| Spikes | Enabled | true | Toggle spike placement |
| Spikes | MinUpDot | 0.7 | Minimum surface normal.y |
| Spikes | Spacing | 0.75 | Minimum gap between spikes |
| Spikes | MaxSpikesPerRoom | 800 | Performance cap |
| Spikes | ScanDelaySeconds | 1.5 | Wait after load before scanning |
| Resources | SpikeDamagePerSecond | 5 | Damage on spike ground |
| Resources | StaminaRegenPerSecOnSpikes | 0.12 | Grip regen per second (0–1 scale) |
| Resources | HealthRegenDelay | 1.5 | Delay before off-spike healing |
| Resources | HealthRegenPerSec | 3 | Health regen after delay |
| Ground | RequireNoHandGrip | false | Require free hands for spike ground |
| UI | ShowHealthBar | true | Show health overlay |

## Build from source

```bash
dotnet build "SpikeBalanceMod.csproj" -c Release -p:GameRoot="C:\Path\To\White Knuckle"
```

Game DLLs are read from `GameRoot/White Knuckle_Data/Managed` and are not redistributed.

## API notes

See [API_NOTES.md](API_NOTES.md) for discovered game types (`ENT_Player`, `GameEntity`, `Damageable.DamageInfo`, etc.).

content.text:
The **Spike Balance Mod** is implemented and built. Here is what was delivered:

## Project location

- **Source:** `c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle Mod\SpikeBalanceMod\`
- **Deployed plugin:** `c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle\BepInEx\plugins\SpikeBalanceMod.dll`
- **BepInEx:** `HideManagerGameObject = true` in `BepInEx\config\BepInEx.cfg`

## Features

| Feature | Implementation |
|--------|----------------|
| **Spikes on up-facing surfaces** | `SpikeSurfaceScanner` samples mesh/box/terrain colliders (`normal.y >= MinUpDot`), dedupes by grid spacing, caps at `MaxSpikesPerRoom` |
| **Spike damage** | `PlayerResourceController` applies DPS via `ENT_Player.Damage` + `Damageable.DamageInfo` while on spike ground |
| **Health bar** | `HealthBarUI` overlay reads `GameEntity.health` / `maxHealth` |
| **Stamina on spikes** | Adds to each hand’s `gripStrength` (0–1) while grounded on spikes |
| **Health off spikes** | Regenerates after `HealthRegenDelay` when not on spike ground |
| **Level load** | `SceneManager.sceneLoaded` + delayed scan for procedural rooms |

## Build & config

```bash
dotnet build "c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle Mod\SpikeBalanceMod\SpikeBalanceMod.csproj" -c Release
```

Config is created on first run: `BepInEx\config\com.spikebalance.whiteknuckle.cfg`

Docs: [README.md](c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle Mod\SpikeBalanceMod\README.md), [API_NOTES.md](c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle Mod\SpikeBalanceMod\API_NOTES.md)

## How to play-test

1. Launch **White Knuckle** from the game folder (Steam / local install).
2. Confirm `BepInEx\LogOutput.log` contains `Spike Balance Mod loaded`.
3. Start a run — after ~1.5s, spikes should appear on upward-facing surfaces.
4. Stand on spikes: health bar drops, grip (stamina) recovers.
5. Climb off spikes: damage stops; after the delay, health recovers.
6. Use **cheat mode** during modded play so leaderboards/stats are not affected ([Steam guide](https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3473004632)).

If the mod does not load, check that `winhttp.dll` and `doorstop_config.ini` are in the game root and that only `SpikeBalanceMod.dll` is in `BepInEx\plugins\`.

## Tuning tips

- Too many spikes → lower `MaxSpikesPerRoom` or raise `Spacing`
- Ramps missing spikes → lower `MinUpDot` (e.g. `0.5`)
- Stamina too slow/fast → adjust `StaminaRegenPerSecOnSpikes` (grip is 0–1, default `0.12`/sec)
- Spike ground while hanging → set `RequireNoHandGrip = true` in config

All plan todos are complete. If you want changes (e.g. Harmony hook on `HandStamina`, reuse of an in-game hazard prefab, or Thunderstore packaging), say what you prefer next.

content.text:
Fixed several issues that explain why the mod loaded but nothing appeared in-game.

## Root causes

1. **Spike scan never ran reliably** — `StartCoroutine` was called from `Configure()` during plugin `Awake()`, often before the runtime `MonoBehaviour` was enabled, so the scan coroutine could silently never run. Your log had no `Placed X spikes` line at all.

2. **Campaign rooms may not fire `sceneLoaded`** — Procedural level geometry can spawn after load, so a single 1.5s scan on menu/empty scenes finds nothing.

3. **Health bar UI was invisible** — Unity `Image` components without a **sprite** render as blank. The bar existed but you couldn’t see it.

4. **Player lookup too narrow** — `FindObjectOfType` only finds active objects; the player may not be found during parts of a run.

## Fixes (v1.0.1)

- Moved scanning to `Start()` with **retry scans** at 2s, 5s, 10s, 20s, 45s after load
- **Re-scan when the player first appears** and every 30s if still 0 spikes
- Use `Resources.FindObjectsOfTypeAll` for colliders/renderers (includes inactive scene objects)
- **Renderer bounds fallback** when few colliders are found
- Spike root uses `DontDestroyOnLoad` so spikes persist across room loads
- Health bar uses a proper **white UI sprite**
- Health bar stays visible (gray) even before the player spawns

Updated to **v1.0.2** — spikes and the health bar only activate during an active campaign run.

## What changed

The mod was using “player exists” and startup timers, which fire on the **main menu** (preview player / early geometry). It now uses the game’s own run state:

- `CL_GameManager.IsInGame()` — run is active  
- `CL_GameManager.IsLoading()` — still loading  
- `runHasEnded` — run finished  
- `localPlayer` — actual run player (not menu preview)

## Behavior now

| Location | Health bar | Spikes |
|----------|------------|--------|
| Main menu | Hidden | None |
| Loading | Hidden | None |
| Active run | Visible | Placed ~5s after run starts, with retries at 3s / 8s / 15s / 25s |
| Run ended | Hidden | Cleared |

Default scan delay is **5 seconds after the run starts** (was 3s at boot).

## What you should see

1. Main menu — **no** health bar  
2. Start campaign — log: `Run started — enabling spike balance mod gameplay`  
3. A few seconds into the first room — log with `placed=...` and spikes on floors  
4. Return to menu — log: `Run ended — hiding UI and clearing spikes`

content.text:
**v1.0.3** is built and deployed. Here’s what was wrong and what changed:

## Fixes

### Health bar not showing
`IsInActiveRun()` was too strict (`IsInGame()` + `IsLoading()` never became true in campaign), so the mod never entered “run” mode.

**Now:** Run detection uses `localPlayer` / `FindPlayer` with health > 0 in a non-menu scene, without requiring `IsInGame()`.

### Health bar layout
- **Top center**, 8px from the top  
- **Health number** centered on the bar (e.g. `87`)  
- Higher canvas sort order (8000)

### Spikes not placing
Walkable surfaces in White Knuckle are mostly **handholds** (`CL_Handhold`), which we were **excluding**. Colliders alone often weren’t enough.

**Now spikes are placed from:**
1. All **`CL_Handhold`** instances + `HandholdManager.handholds` list  
2. **Raycast grid** (~40m) around the player downward onto surfaces  
3. Colliders + mesh renderers (handholds no longer excluded from collider scan)

Other tweaks: default `MinUpDot` **0.45**, larger spike visuals, spikes parented to the **active scene** (not only DontDestroyOnLoad).

## What you should see

1. **Menu** — no health bar  
2. **Campaign run** — health bar at **top center** with numeric HP  
3. **Log** lines like:
   ```
   Run started — enabling spike balance mod gameplay
   Spike scan ... handholds=120 placements=... placed=...
   ```
4. **Red spike cylinders** on floors/handholds near you

Rebuild is in `BepInEx\plugins\SpikeBalanceMod.dll`. Start a campaign run and check the log. If `placed=0` still appears, paste the full `Spike scan` log line (with `handholds=` and `colliders=` counts).

content.text:
**v1.0.4** is deployed. Three bugs were blocking everything:

## Root causes

1. **Runtime never ticked** — `SpikeBalanceRuntime` lived on a hidden `HideAndDontSave` GameObject, so `Update()` never ran (log had no `Run started`, no scans).
2. **Scene filter too broad** — menu detection included `"Main"`, which likely matched your **level scene name** and blocked the whole run.
3. **Player gate too strict** — required `maxHealth > 0`, which may be 0 until the run fully starts.

## Fixes in v1.0.4

| Change | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| Runtime on **BepInEx plugin** `gameObject` | `Update` / coroutines always run |
| Removed `HideFlags.HideAndDontSave` | No hidden broken object |
| Menu scenes: only `MainMenu`, `Title`, etc. (no `"Main"`) | Campaign scenes are allowed |
| **Harmony** on `ENT_Player.Start` | Triggers scan when player spawns |
| Health bar always on during run | Top-center + HP number |
| **DebugLog = true** by default | State logged every 8s |

## After updating

1. Confirm `BepInEx\plugins\SpikeBalanceMod.dll` is recent (v1.0.4).
2. Start a **campaign run**.
3. Open `BepInEx\LogOutput.log` — you should see lines like:

```
SpikeBalanceRuntime Start — Update loop active.
ENT_Player.Start detected — scene=...
Run started — spikes + health bar enabled.
Spike scan ... placed=...
Debug: inRun=True ...
```

4. Health bar at **top center**; spikes after a few seconds.

If it still fails, paste the **full log** from one menu → run session (especially any `Debug:` and `Spike scan` lines). Set `DebugLog = true` in `BepInEx\config\com.spikebalance.whiteknuckle.cfg` if you turned it off.

input.contents:
# Spike Balance Mod v2.0.0

BepInEx plugin for **White Knuckle**.

## Features

- **Spikes** on upward surfaces (handholds, raycasts, colliders) after a run starts
- **Health bar** at top-center (8px from top) with numeric HP
- **On spike ground:** stamina (grip) recovers, health drains
- **Off spike ground:** health regen after a short delay

## Install

1. Install [BepInEx 5 x64](https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases) in the game folder.
2. Build: `dotnet build -c Release` (copies DLL to `White Knuckle/BepInEx/plugins/`).
3. Launch the game. Check `BepInEx/LogOutput.log` for `Spike Balance Mod v2.0.0`.

## Config

`BepInEx/config/com.spikebalance.whiteknuckle.cfg`

## Leaderboards

Enable in-game **cheat mode** when using mods (see Steam modding guide).

## Build

```bash
dotnet build -c Release -p:GameRoot="C:\Path\To\White Knuckle"
```

# API Notes (White Knuckle)

- `ENT_Player` : `GameEntity` — fields `health`, `maxHealth`
- `ENT_Player.Hand` — field `gripStrength` (0–1 stamina)
- `ENT_Player.Damage(DamageInfo)` — `Damageable.DamageInfo` nested type (`amount`, `type`)
- `CL_GameManager` — `localPlayer`, `runHasEnded`; static `IsInGame()`, `IsLoading()`
- `CL_Handhold` — climb surfaces; primary spike placement target
- `HandholdManager.handholds` — list of active handholds

Run detection: active `localPlayer` / `ENT_Player` in a non-menu scene, `runHasEnded == false`.

Rebuilt the mod from scratch as **v2.0.0** with a simpler design based on what failed before.

## What was wrong before

- Extra hidden `GameObject` + `HideFlags` → **`Update()` never ran** (no log lines after “loaded”)
- Scene filter blocked levels named like `Main`
- Too many layers (runtime component, run state classes) made debugging hard

## New architecture (v2.0.0)

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| [`SpikeBalancePlugin.cs`](c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle Mod\SpikeBalanceMod\SpikeBalancePlugin.cs) | **All logic on `BaseUnityPlugin`** — `Update` / `FixedUpdate` always run |
| [`Core/PlayerBridge.cs`](c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle Mod\SpikeBalanceMod\Core\PlayerBridge.cs) | Health, grip, damage, player lookup |
| [`Spikes/SpikeSystem.cs`](c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle Mod\SpikeBalanceMod\Spikes\SpikeSystem.cs) | Handholds + raycasts + colliders; retries at 2/5/10/20s |
| [`UI/HealthBar.cs`](c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle Mod\SpikeBalanceMod\UI\HealthBar.cs) | Top-center bar, **8px from top**, **HP number centered** |
| [`Patches/PlayerSpawnPatch.cs`](c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle Mod\SpikeBalanceMod\Patches\PlayerSpawnPatch.cs) | Triggers run when `ENT_Player.Start` fires |

## Your requested features

- **Health bar:** top middle, 8px margin, current HP as text in the bar center  
- **Spikes:** on upward surfaces via handholds (main WK surfaces), raycasts around player, collider tops  
- **Balance:** on spikes → grip up, health down; off spikes → health regen after delay  
- **Menu:** no bar, no spikes until run starts  

## Install

`c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle\BepInEx\plugins\SpikeBalanceMod.dll`

content.text:
**v2.0.2** is deployed with stronger separation between mod health and grip.

## Why grip still dropped

Two things were still coupling them:

1. **Writing to the game `health` field** (`SyncToGameHealth`) — the game appears to treat health loss as something that can pull down grip (via `DamageGripStrength` or similar).
2. **`DamageGripStrength`** — even without `Damage()`, other code can still drain grip when health changes.

## What changed

| Change | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **Mod health is fully separate** | Spike damage/regen only change the mod pool shown on your bar; the game `health` field is not updated each tick |
| **Harmony block on `DamageGripStrength`** | While mod health is updating, that method is skipped |
| **Grip snapshot / restore** | Before spike damage, grip is saved; after damage, it’s restored, then stamina regen is applied |

On spikes the order is now:

1. Save grip  
2. Apply mod health damage (no game `Damage()`, no health field write)  
3. Restore grip  
4. Apply stamina regen (`AddGrip`)

Rebuild is in `BepInEx\plugins\SpikeBalanceMod.dll`.

Stand on spikes again: the **health bar** should drop while **hand stamina (grip) only goes up** from the regen, not down from damage.

If grip still drops, tell me whether it happens on **spikes only** or also when you take **normal fall/vanilla damage** — we can add a similar patch for vanilla `Damage()` if needed.

content.text:
**v2.1.0** is built and deployed. Here’s what was going wrong:

## Root cause

Grip wasn’t dropping through `ENT_Player.Damage()` or our mod health. The game drains stamina via:

**`ENT_Player.Hand.DamageGripStrength(float)`**

called from **`HandStamina`** while you’re on surfaces (including spike-covered handholds). We had only patched `ENT_Player.DamageGripStrength`, not the nested **`Hand`** method.

## Fix (v2.1.0)

1. **Harmony patch on `Hand.DamageGripStrength`** — blocks grip drain while on mod spike ground  
2. **Block `SetGripStrength`** on spikes — stops other grip reductions  
3. **`HandStamina` postfix** — after the game runs, restores grip to a per-contact floor  
4. **Grip floor** — when you step on spikes, current grip is saved; it may only go **up** (from regen), not down  
5. **`LateUpdate` enforcement** — runs after game logic each frame while on spikes  

Mod health stays separate; stamina regen on spikes still uses `AddGrip` directly.

## What you should see

On spikes only:

- **Health bar (mod HP)** goes down  
- **Hand stamina (grip)** stays the same or **increases** from regen — it should not drop  

Log line every 10s includes `grip0=...` and `onSpikes=True` for debugging.

DLL: `BepInEx\plugins\SpikeBalanceMod.dll` (v2.1.0). Try a run and stand on spikes again. If grip still drops, note whether you’re **hanging** vs **standing** on spikes — that helps tune `HandStamina` further.

# API Notes (White Knuckle)

## Player

- `ENT_Player` : `GameEntity` — vanilla `health` / `maxHealth` (not used by this mod for spike damage)
- `ENT_Player.Hand` — `gripStrength` (0–1), `AddGripStrength`, `SetGripStrength`, `DamageGripStrength`, `MinGripStrength`
- `ENT_Player.HandStamina(Int32, Hand, Boolean, Boolean, Single)` — main stamina drain; skipped on spike ground
- `ENT_Player.DamageGripStrength(Single, Boolean)` — blocked on spike ground
- `ENT_Player.infiniteStamina` — set `true` on spike ground to suppress drain
- `ENT_Player.Damage(DamageInfo)` — never called for spike damage (mod health pool only)

## World

- `CL_Handhold` / `HandholdManager.handholds` — primary walk/climb surfaces for spike placement
- `CL_GameManager.localPlayer`, `runHasEnded`

## Run detection

Active `ENT_Player` in a non-menu scene, `runHasEnded == false`.

# Spike Balance Mod v3.0.0

BepInEx 5 plugin for **White Knuckle**. Full rewrite focused on isolating mod health from grip/stamina.

## Features

- **Spikes** on upward surfaces (handholds, raycasts, colliders) after a run starts
- **Health bar** at top-center (8px from top) with numeric mod HP
- **On spike ground:** grip recovers; **mod health** drains (vanilla grip drain disabled)
- **Off spike ground:** mod health regen after a delay; vanilla `HandStamina` runs normally

## Grip protection on spikes

1. Sets `infiniteStamina` while on spike ground
2. Skips `HandStamina` entirely
3. Blocks `DamageGripStrength` / `MinGripStrength` on player and hands
4. Regenerates via `Hand.AddGripStrength` and enforces a per-hand floor in `LateUpdate`

Spike damage never calls `ENT_Player.Damage` — only the mod health pool.

## Install

1. [BepInEx 5 x64](https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases) in the game folder (`HideManagerGameObject = true`).
2. `dotnet build -c Release` (copies to `White Knuckle/BepInEx/plugins/`).
3. Launch and check `BepInEx/LogOutput.log` for `Spike Balance Mod v3.0.0`.

## Config

`BepInEx/config/com.spikebalance.whiteknuckle.cfg`

Set `Debug.LogStatus = true` to log `modHp` and `grip0` every 2 seconds.

## Leaderboards

Use in-game **cheat mode** when running mods.

**Spike Balance Mod v3.0.0** is a full rewrite. The old source tree was removed and replaced with a smaller, purpose-built layout.

## What changed

**Architecture**
- All gameplay runs on `BaseUnityPlugin` (no hidden `GameObject` — that broke `Update` before).
- **Mod health** is fully separate: spike damage never calls `ENT_Player.Damage()`.
- **Grip/stamina on spikes** uses four layers (the main fix from API work):

| Layer | What it does |
|--------|----------------|
| `infiniteStamina = true` | Game’s built-in no-drain flag while on spike ground |
| Skip `HandStamina` | Prefix returns false — no climbing strain drain |
| Block `DamageGripStrength` / `MinGripStrength` | On both `ENT_Player` and `Hand` |
| `AddGripStrength` + floor enforce | Regen in `FixedUpdate`, clamp in `LateUpdate` |

**Spikes**
- Markers now have **trigger sphere colliders** so ground detection actually hits them (this was missing before).
- Placement: handholds → raycast grid → nearby colliders; rescans at 2s / 5s / 10s / 20s.

**UI**
- Top-center bar, 8px margin, numeric **mod HP** only.

## Deployed build

`White Knuckle\BepInEx\plugins\SpikeBalanceMod.dll` (built and copied automatically).

## How to test

1. Launch the game and start a run.
2. In `BepInEx/LogOutput.log`, confirm: `Spike Balance Mod v3.0.0 loaded` and `Run started in …`.
3. Optional: set `Debug.LogStatus = true` in `BepInEx/config/com.spikebalance.whiteknuckle.cfg` — logs `modHp` and `grip0` every 2s.
4. Stand on spike surfaces: **mod HP** should drop; **grip0** should stay flat or rise, not fall.
5. Climb off spikes: grip behaves normally; mod HP regens after the delay.

If grip still drops on spikes, tell me whether **grip0** in the log drops too — that separates “vanilla UI shows something else” from “our patches aren’t firing.”

content.text:
Spike placement was effectively disabled by the last filter pass. Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

## Root causes

1. **Name filter was too aggressive** — excluding names containing `"box"` also matched things like **Skybox**, which could reject large parts of the level hierarchy.
2. **Handhold colliders are triggers** — the placer skipped all trigger colliders, but White Knuckle uses triggers for climb/walk surfaces, so almost nothing was sampled.
3. **Raycast rules were too tight** — required a precise non-trigger hit on the same collider, which rarely succeeded.

## Fix (rebuilt & deployed)

Spikes are placed only on **level geometry** still, but via methods that actually work in this game:

- **Direct placement** on each `CL_Handhold` with an upward-facing normal
- **Grid on handhold bounds** (includes trigger colliders + renderers)
- **Raycast grid** that only accepts hits under a `CL_Handhold` (or terrain), with `QueryTriggerInteraction.Collide`
- **Extra scan at 15s** for late-loaded handholds
- **Default `MinUpDot` lowered to 0.45** (config file keeps your old value until you change it)

After starting a run, check `BepInEx/LogOutput.log` for lines like:

`Spike scan: 842 handholds, 312 markers (+120)`

- If **handholds = 0**, the level hadn’t loaded handholds yet — wait ~15s or restart the run.
- If **handholds > 0 but markers = 0**, lower `MinUpDot` in config (try `0.35`).

Props/boxes should stay excluded because they don’t use `CL_Handhold`. If anything still spikes on an object, tell me what it is and we can tighten further without breaking floors again.

input.contents:
# White Knuckle level geometry (spike placement)

## What a “level” is made of

Rooms are not generic mesh floors. **Climbable/walkable surfaces are `CL_Handhold` components** loaded by `HandholdManager.LoadHandholds` into `HandholdManager.handholds`.

| Object | Role | Spikes? |
|--------|------|--------|
| **`CL_Handhold`** (default) | Floor, wall, ledge, ceiling climb volumes | **Floor only** (normal ≈ up) |
| **`CL_Handhold_Rope`** | Rope swing lines | No |
| **`CL_Handhold_Icy` / `_Breakable` / `_Damage`** | Special climb surfaces | Floor only, same normal rules |
| **`HandholdModule_LimitDirection`** | Defines surface **`normal`** + angle limit | Use `normal` to classify floor vs wall |
| **TerrainCollider** | Rare outdoor terrain | Yes (upward hit normal) |
| **Props / boxes** | `CL_Prop`, rigidbodies — no handhold | No |

## Floor vs wall (why you took spike damage on walls)

- **Wall handholds** use a surface normal pointing **horizontally** (`normal.y ≈ 0`).
- **Floor handholds** use a normal pointing **up** (`normal.y ≥ MinUpDot`, default 0.45).

The previous placer treated **every** `CL_Handhold` as a spike candidate and, for walls, still placed points on the **top of the bounds** using `Vector3.up`. Those markers sat above/near wall grips, so hanging on the wall could overlap spike trigger spheres.

## What we attach spikes to (v3.1+)

1. Handholds classified as **`HandholdKind.Floor`** via `HandholdSurface` (`LimitDirection.normal` or `transform.up`).
2. Optional terrain raycasts.
3. **Not** walls, ceilings, ropes, or props.

Enable `Debug.LogStatus = true` in config to log per scene:

`[SceneName] handholds: floor=… wall=… ceiling=… rope=… | spike markers=…`

# API Notes (White Knuckle)

## Player

- `ENT_Player` : `GameEntity` — vanilla `health` / `maxHealth` (not used by mod spike damage)
- `ENT_Player.Hand` — `gripStrength`, `AddGripStrength`, `DamageGripStrength`
- `ENT_Player.HandStamina` — skipped on spike ground
- `ENT_Player.infiniteStamina` — toggled on spike ground

## Level geometry (spikes)

- **`CL_Handhold`** — all climb/walk surfaces; only **floor** instances (up-facing normal) get spikes
- **`HandholdModule_LimitDirection.normal`** — authoritative surface normal for classification
- **`CL_Handhold_Rope`** — excluded
- **`HandholdManager.handholds`** — runtime list of active handholds in the room

See `LEVEL_GEOMETRY.md` for full breakdown.

## Run detection

Active `ENT_Player` in a non-menu scene, `runHasEnded == false`.

content.text:
Dissected the game’s level model (via `Assembly-CSharp` + handhold API) and fixed the wall-damage bug. Here’s what levels actually use and what changed.

## How White Knuckle levels are built

Levels are **not** “mesh floor + props.” Walkable/climbable surfaces are almost all **`CL_Hhold` volumes** registered on `HandholdManager.handholds` when the room loads.

| Component | What it is | Spikes should attach? |
|-----------|------------|------------------------|
| **`CL_Handhold`** | Floors, ledges, **walls**, ceilings | **Only if surface faces up** |
| **`HandholdModule_LimitDirection`** | `normal` + `maxAngle` — real surface orientation | Used to tell floor vs wall |
| **`CL_Handhold_Rope`** | Ropes | **No** |
| **`CL_Handhold_Icy` / `_Breakable` / `_Damage`** | Special surfaces | Floor only (same normal rule) |
| **Terrain** | Rare | Yes (upward hits) |
| **Props / boxes** | `CL_Prop`, rigidbodies — **no** handhold | **No** |

So the correct spike targets are **floor-class handholds** (and terrain), not every handhold in the room.

## Why you took spike damage on walls

Wall grips use handholds whose normal is **horizontal** (`normal.y ≈ 0`). The old placer still:

1. Treated **all** handholds as valid, and  
2. For walls, fell back to **`Vector3.up`** and placed markers on the **top of the wall’s bounds** — right where you hang.

Those trigger spheres overlapped your wall grip even though you weren’t on a floor.

## Fix (v3.1.0 — deployed)

- **`HandholdSurface`** classifies each handhold: `Floor` / `Wall` / `Ceiling` / `Rope` using `HandholdModule_LimitDirection.normal` (or `transform.up`).
- Spikes only on **`HandholdKind.Floor`** (`normal.y >= MinUpDot`).
- **Ropes excluded**; wall/ceiling handholds skipped entirely.
- Survey log when scans run (or with `Debug.LogStatus = true`):

  `[RoomName] handholds: floor=120 wall=380 ceiling=12 rope=8 | spike markers=95`

Details are in `LEVEL_GEOMETRY.md` in the mod folder.

## What you should see in logs

After starting a run, something like:

```text
[SomeRoom] handholds: floor=… wall=… ceiling=… rope=… | spike markers=…
```

- **`wall` high, `floor` moderate** — expected in vertical rooms.  
- **`markers` should be much lower than before** and only track `floor`.  
- Hanging a **wall** handhold should **not** tick mod HP unless you’re also on a floor spike zone nearby.

If wall damage still happens occasionally, note the room name and whether you’re above a floor ledge — we can tighten `MinUpDot` (e.g. `0.55`) in config.

# Spike placement — level geometry only

## Decoupled from handholds

`CL_Handhold` volumes are **climb helpers** (walls, ledges, ropes). They are **not** used for spike placement.

Spikes are placed by raycasting onto **static level colliders**:

- `MeshCollider`, `BoxCollider`, `TerrainCollider`, etc.
- **Non-trigger**, no non-kinematic `Rigidbody`
- **Not** under a `CL_Handhold` parent
- Surface normal within **20° of horizontal** (configurable `MaxSurfaceTiltDegrees`)

## Placement position

Hit point from physics, then shifted **into** the mesh by `SurfaceInset` (default 6 cm) so markers sit inside geometry instead of floating outside rounded walls.

## Damage while hanging on walls

Spike ground checks that the marker is **under your feet** (limited horizontal offset), so brushing a wall handhold should not count unless you are actually over a floor spike zone.

# API Notes (White Knuckle)

## Player

- `CL_Handhold` — climb volumes; **excluded** from spike placement

## Spike placement (v3.2)

content.text:
**v3.2.0** decouples spikes from handholds and places them on real level geometry.

## Changes

**Placement (no handholds)**
- Raycast grid hits **static mesh/box/terrain colliders** only
- **Skips** anything under `CL_Handhold`, triggers, non-kinematic rigidbodies, and the player
- **Tilt limit:** `MaxSurfaceTiltDegrees = 20` → only surfaces within 20° of flat (`normal.y ≥ cos(20°) ≈ 0.94`)
- **Inset:** `SurfaceInset = 0.06` pushes markers **into** the surface so they don’t float outside rounded walls

**Damage while wall-hanging**
- Spike ground only counts if the marker is **under your feet** (small horizontal tolerance), not beside you on a wall

**Smaller trigger spheres** so clips into geometry are less likely to overlap you on vertical surfaces

## Config (`BepInEx/config/com.spikebalance.whiteknuckle.cfg`)

| Key | Default | Meaning |
|-----|---------|---------|
| `MaxSurfaceTiltDegrees` | `20` | Max slope for spike surfaces |
| `SurfaceInset` | `0.06` | How far markers sit inside the mesh |

Remove old `MinUpDot` from config if it’s still there — it’s replaced by tilt degrees.

## What to expect

- Log: `Spike scan (geometry): tilt<=20° minUp=0.940 markers=…`
- Standing on floors/ledges: damage on upward mesh
- Hanging on **wall handholds only**: should **not** take spike damage unless your feet are over an actual floor spike zone

If you see `markers=0` in a room, that level may only use handhold triggers without separate floor meshes — tell me which room and we can add a safe mesh-only fallback without re-coupling to climb volumes.

content.text:
**v3.3.0** is deployed. Here’s what was wrong and what changed.

## Why logs showed markers but you felt nothing

1. **Markers were inside solid mesh** (`SurfaceInset`) — your feet hit the floor collider; the spike trigger never overlapped the player.
2. **Raycast detection hit the floor mesh first**, not the spike trigger sitting beside/under it.
3. **No visuals** — invisible trigger spheres only.

## Fixes

| Change | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| **Above-surface placement** | `SurfaceOffsetAbove` (default 4 cm) + trigger sits on the walkable surface |
| **Proximity detection** | Checks distance from your feet to each marker (registry), not physics through the floor |
| **Red spike visuals** | Small red cylinders + pad on each zone (`ShowSpikeVisuals = true` by default) |
| **Larger contact radius** | Easier to stand in a zone and take damage |

## What you should see now

- **Red spike clusters** on upward floors/ledges (≤20° tilt)
- **Mod HP drops** when standing on them (`spikes=true` in debug log)
- Debug log also shows `markers=N` while `spikes=true/false` when you step on/off zones

## Config

```ini
[Spikes]
ShowSpikeVisuals = true
SurfaceOffsetAbove = 0.04
MaxSurfaceTiltDegrees = 20
```

If you still see markers in log but no red meshes, set `ShowSpikeVisuals = true` explicitly (old config may lack the key). If visuals appear but no damage, enable `Debug.LogStatus = true` and check whether `spikes` flips to `true` when standing on red pads.

content.text:
**v3.4.0** is deployed with two fixes:

## 1. No damage on visible spikes

The player root sits around **chest height**, but detection treated that as “feet.” Markers on the floor were ~1.5 m below, so the check always skipped them (`delta.y > 0.85`).

**Fix:** `GetFeetPosition()` raycasts down to the ground under you. Damage checks use that point and whether your feet are **above** the marker (0–1.75 m), not the old inverted logic. Slightly larger horizontal tolerance (`GroundSphereRadius` default **0.65**).

## 2. Only first room had spikes

Scans ran **once at run start** near room 1. Room 2 loaded later with no new scan; the coroutine had already finished.

**Fix:**
- Rescan when **`HandholdManager.LoadHandholds`** runs (new room geometry)
- Rescan when **`CL_GameManager.LoadScene`** runs
- Rescan when the **Unity scene name** changes
- Each rescan **clears old markers** and raycasts around your **current** position (only colliders in the active scene)

## What to check

1. Enter room 2 — log should show: `Room geometry loaded — scanning…` and `Spike scan [SceneName]: markers=…`
2. Walk onto red spike pads — mod HP should drop; with `Debug.LogStatus = true`, `spikes=true`
3. If room 2 still has no spikes, check the log line’s **player coordinates** — you may need to walk in a bit before the scan runs (~1–8 s after load)

Config tip: if detection still feels tight, raise `GroundSphereRadius` to `0.8` in `BepInEx/config/com.spikebalance.whiteknuckle.cfg`.

**v3.5.0** is deployed. Two separate bugs were causing what you saw.

## Missing health UI

During room transitions the player often disappears for a moment. The mod treated that as “run over” and called `EndRun()`, which hid the bar and reset run state.

**Fix:** The run stays active until you’re actually in a menu or `runHasEnded` is true. Short gaps with no player no longer end the run — the health bar stays up and keeps showing mod HP.

## No spikes in room 2

1. **Rescan cleared room 1 markers** and scans could run while `ModState.InRun` was false → placement skipped.  
2. **Same Unity scene** for both rooms — only a scene-name change triggered a rescan, so room 2 never got scanned.  
3. **Strict scene filter** on raycast hits could reject valid room geometry.

**Fix:**
- **Additive scans** when you move ~32 m into a new area (typical second room), without wiping room 1 markers first  
- **`HandholdManager.LoadHandholds`** triggers an additive scan for each room  
- **Position-based rescan** when you move far from the last scan point  
- Raycasts no longer require the hit collider to be in the “same scene” as the player  
- Markers are parented into the player’s active scene  

## What you should see

1. **Health bar** for the whole run (including room 2).  
2. Log when entering room 2: `New room area (x,y,z) — rescanning spikes` or `Room load — additive spike scan`.  
3. Then: `Spike scan: markers=N (+M)` with **M > 0**.  
4. Red spike visuals in room 2 after ~0.5–8 s.

If room 2 still shows `markers=0 (+0)`, the room may have no non-handhold floor meshes (only climb triggers). Tell me the log line and we can add a safe mesh fallback without tying spikes to handholds again.

**v3.6.0** is deployed. Here’s what was wrong and what changed.

## No damage on visible spikes

Distance checks from “feet” were missing the trigger zones. Detection now uses **`Collider.ClosestPoint`** on each spike sphere against your body and feet — the standard way to test overlap with triggers when the player has no rigidbody.

- Default **`SpikeTouchDistance`** (was `GroundSphereRadius`) = **0.85 m**
- Markers sit **on** the surface (`SurfaceOffsetAbove = 0.02`)

With `Debug.LogStatus = true`, watch **`nearestSpike`**:
- **< 0.85** and **`spikes=true`** → working
- **< 0.85** but **`spikes=false`** → tell me (shouldn’t happen now)
- **> 2** while standing on red pads → markers aren’t where you’re walking

## Not continuous / 2nd floor sparse

1. **400 marker cap** filled room 1 → room 2 got almost nothing → cap raised to **1200** with pruning of zones **90 m+** behind you  
2. **One scan burst** at room entry only → **continuous scans** every **3 s** or every **6 m** moved (additive, fills as you explore)  
3. **2nd floor** missed by single ray height → **3 ray heights** (8 m / 18 m / 30 m above you) per grid point  
4. **Tighter grid** (spacing default **2 m**, step ≈ 0.9 m)

## What you should see

- Red pads **filling in** as you walk through a room (not only at the start)  
- **2nd floor** gets its own patches after you go up  
- **Mod HP drops** on red pads; grip still recovers on spikes  

Config knobs in `com.spikebalance.whiteknuckle.cfg`:

```ini
[Spikes]
Spacing = 2
MaxPerRun = 1200
ContinuousScanInterval = 3
ContinuousScanMoveDistance = 6
[Detection]
SpikeTouchDistance = 0.85
```

Enable debug logging and stand on a spike: `nearestSpike` should be under ~0.85 and `spikes=true`.

# Spike Balance Mod v4.0.0

Clean rewrite for **White Knuckle** (BepInEx 5).

## Features

- **Spikes** on upward-facing **level mesh** (≤20° tilt), not handhold climb volumes
- **Continuous scans** every 2.5s / 5m traveled; rescan on `LoadHandholds` (new rooms)
- **Red spike visuals** on each zone
- **Mod health bar** (top center, numeric HP) — separate from vanilla HP
- **On spikes:** mod HP drains, **grip recovers** (no vanilla grip drain)
- **Off spikes:** mod HP regen after delay, vanilla stamina

## Install

Build copies to `White Knuckle/BepInEx/plugins/SpikeBalanceMod.dll`.

Set `HideManagerGameObject = true` in `BepInEx/config/BepInEx.cfg`.

## Config

`BepInEx/config/com.spikebalance.whiteknuckle.cfg`

Key values: `TouchDistance` (default 1.0), `Spacing` (1.8), `ScanInterval` (2.5), `ShowVisuals` (true).

Enable `Debug.LogStatus` to see `onSpikes`, `near=` distance in log.

## Cheat mode

Use in-game cheat mode when running mods (Steam guide).

# White Knuckle — mod API notes

- **Level floors:** static non-trigger colliders, not under `CL_Handhold`
- **Handholds:** climb only; trigger `LoadHandholds` for room changes
- **Grip:** `Hand.gripStrength`, `AddGripStrength`, block `HandStamina` / `DamageGripStrength` on spikes
- **Damage:** mod `HealthPool` only — never `ENT_Player.Damage`

**Spike Balance Mod v4.0.0** is a full rewrite. All prior source files were removed and replaced with a small, focused layout.

## New structure (11 source files)

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `Plugin.cs` | Main loop on `BaseUnityPlugin` |
| `Config.cs` | BepInEx settings |
| `GameRefs.cs` | Player, feet, grip, run checks |
| `HealthPool.cs` / `HealthBar.cs` | Mod HP + top UI |
| `Grip.cs` | Stamina on spikes |
| `Spikes/SpikeField.cs` | Scan, spawn, detect, prune |
| `Spikes/SpikeZone.cs` | Zone + `ClosestPoint` touch test |
| `Spikes/LevelGeom.cs` | Upward floor mesh filter (no handholds) |
| `Patches.cs` | Harmony (grip + room load) |

Deployed to `White Knuckle\BepInEx\plugins\SpikeBalanceMod.dll`.

## Your spec (implemented)

- **Spikes:** upward level mesh only (≤20°), not handholds/props  
- **Continuous:** scan every **2.5 s** and every **5 m** moved; extra scans on room load + boot burst  
- **Visuals:** red pegs on each zone  
- **Health bar:** top center, mod HP only  
- **On spikes:** mod HP down, grip up (blocks `HandStamina` / grip damage, uses `infiniteStamina` + `AddGripStrength`)  
- **Off spikes:** mod HP regen after delay  
- **Run:** stays active through room loads (only ends on menu / run end)

## Damage detection (simplified)

`SpikeZone.Touches()` uses **`Collider.ClosestPoint`** on body + feet with **`TouchDistance = 1.0`** — no raycast-through-floor issues.

## Testing

1. Delete or rename old config so new defaults apply: `BepInEx/config/com.spikebalance.whiteknuckle.cfg`  
2. Launch → log: `Spike Balance Mod 4.0.0`  
3. Start run → health bar visible  
4. Room 2 → log: `Room loaded — scanning spikes`  
5. Enable `Debug.LogStatus = true` → stand on red zone: `onSpikes=true`, `near=0.xx`  

If `near` is small but `onSpikes=false`, tell me. If room 2 shows `zones=0 (+0)`, level mesh may be handhold-only — we can discuss a safe fallback next.

BepInEx plugin — upward-facing level geometry becomes **lava** (kids' game style).

## Features

- **Every room:** when `HandholdManager.LoadHandholds` runs, all upward floor colliders in that room are filled with lava zones (all heights).
- **Mod health bar** (top center) — lava drains mod HP; vanilla grip **recovers** on lava.
- **Off lava:** mod HP regen after a short delay.

## Install

4. `HideManagerGameObject = true` in BepInEx.cfg

## Config

- `Spacing` — lava density (default 1.6)
- `MaxTiltDegrees` — 20° (floor-like surfaces only)
- `MaxZones` — 8000

Enable `Debug.LogStatus` to see `onLava=true` when standing in lava.

**The Floor Is Lava v1.0.0** is built and deployed. Here’s what changed:

## Renamed
- Plugin: **The Floor Is Lava**
- DLL: `BepInEx/plugins/TheFloorIsLava.dll` (old `SpikeBalanceMod.dll` is removed on build)
- Config: `BepInEx/config/com.thefloorislava.whiteknuckle.cfg`

## Why only the first room’s bottom floor before
Spikes were only raycast in a **small box around the player once**. Room 2 never got a full pass, and upper floors were easy to miss.

## New “floor is lava” fill
When each room loads (`HandholdManager.LoadHandholds`):

1. Walk **every collider** under the room root **and** scene roots  
2. Grid-sample the **top** of each valid floor mesh (up to 20° tilt, not handholds)  
3. Run **3 delayed passes** (0.25s, 1s, 2s) so geometry/physics is ready  
4. Light **explore scans** follow the player for gaps (`ExploreRadius`, every 4s / 8m)  
5. Up to **8000** lava zones per run (no pruning that cleared other rooms)

Room loaded: 'RoomName' — filling upward surfaces with lava.
Lava fill 'RoomName': +842 zones (total 1200)
```

## What you should see

- Lava damage still works (unchanged detection)

content.text: [REDACTED]

**The Floor Is Lava v1.0.1** is deployed with a damage fix.

## Cause

Lava zones were **trigger colliders without a Rigidbody**. In Unity, `OnTriggerStay` usually does not run unless the trigger (or the other object) has a **Rigidbody**. With thousands of zones, only doing a manual distance loop was also easy to get wrong for the player’s actual colliders (often hands vs feet).

## Fix

1. **Kinematic Rigidbody** on every lava zone (required for trigger messages).
2. **`OnTriggerStay`** → `LavaContact` reports when the player is in lava.
3. **Flat box trigger** (wide, 0.4m tall) so standing on the floor overlaps reliably, not only a small sphere.
4. **Fallbacks:** `OverlapSphere` at feet + distance check on **nearby** zones only (performance with 1000+ zones).

## What you should see

- Stand on orange lava → **mod HP drops** on the top bar.
- Grip still recovers on lava (unchanged).
- With `Debug.LogStatus = true`: `onLava=true` and `hp` counting down.

Config: `Lava.TouchDistance` default **1.25** (section `[Lava]` in `com.thefloorislava.whiteknuckle.cfg`).

If HP still does not move, enable debug logging and say whether `onLava` stays `false` while `nearest` is near `0`.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>

Going to thoroughly diagnose and fix both problems. Let me first look at the current files in detail.

 I'm looking at two separate bugs the user is experiencing. First, the lava markers aren't dealing damage to the player's health bar even though the collision detection seems to be set up with a trigger collider and contact reporting system. Second, the markers stop spawning after the second floor, which suggests an issue with the generation logic or a hard limit somewhere. Let me trace through the damage system first to see where the health reduction is failing. Checking the overlap sphere logic...

The raycast for feet position ignores triggers and should hit the floor, returning a point 5cm above ground. The lava zone sits 3cm above the floor, so they're only about 2cm apart vertically. With a 1.85m radius sphere centered at the feet position, it should definitely catch the lava zone — unless the feet position calculation is somehow placing it too far away from where the lava actually is.

The lava box collider extends 15cm below its zone position in world space, so it should overlap the feet height. But maybe the issue is that the lava zones aren't actually being spawned, or there's something wrong with how the player's collider interacts with the trigger detection. Let me trace through the Update flow to see where PlayerOnSpikes gets called and what happens with that result.

Looking at the v3.6 version, it used a different approach — iterating through all spike markers and checking distance using ClosestPoint on SphereColliders rather than relying on trigger overlap detection. The user mentioned that version was working, so something about the current implementation must have broken that detection logic. I'm wondering if the player's collider might not be a direct child of the ENT_Player object, which could break the `GetComponentInParent` lookup in the OnTriggerStay callback.

But actually, the `Physics.OverlapSphere` approach should work independently regardless of the player hierarchy — it just finds all colliders within range, so either the lava zones aren't properly registered as physics colliders or the probe position calculation is off.

Looking at the probe placement: it's positioned at floor level horizontally aligned with the player's body, which should be correct. The zone visuals are spawned at the surface position plus a small offset, and the collider is added right after, so that should be fine too. Let me reconsider whether the visuals are actually placed correctly in the physics system.

Actually, I'm wondering if the real issue is the player hierarchy itself — if the hands are separate GameObjects not parented under ENT_Player, and the lava box only overlaps with those hand objects, then `GetComponentInParent<ENT_Player>` might not find the player at all.

The safer approach is to skip the parent lookup entirely and instead check the collider's attached rigidbody directly, or use a pure overlap-based detection at the player's position rather than relying on trigger callbacks.

Looking at `PlayerOnSpikes`, it's already doing an overlap sphere check with a 1.85m radius, which should catch lava zones reliably since the SpikeZone component lives on the same GameObject as the collider. But if the user reports zero damage, either `onSpikes` is staying false or the health bar isn't updating visually even though damage is being applied. I should add debug logging to trace what's actually happening, and then move on to the second issue with generation stopping after the second floor.

For the generation problem, the continuous scan via `ExploreNearPlayer` should keep firing as the player moves 8 meters or every 4 seconds, scanning a 40-meter radius. But if markers only appear in rooms 1 and 2, either the hook isn't being called for later rooms or something about how colliders are retrieved from the room root is failing in those upper sections.

I'm realizing the Harmony patch might be capturing the wrong parameter — if the actual `LoadHandholds` method uses a different parameter name than `roomRoot`, it would receive null, which would break the scan. I should use indexed parameter access like `__0` instead to reliably grab the first argument, or switch to `object[] __args` to be safe. Though if it was null, rooms 1 and 2 probably wouldn't work either unless the fallback `scene.GetRootGameObjects()` is saving those cases.

Looking at the fallback logic, even if `roomRoot` is null, the code iterates through all root GameObjects in the scene, which should theoretically find geometry in any room. But if White Knuckle streams rooms as separate prefabs into the same scene, then `GetRootGameObjects()` would scan every collider in every loaded room each time `LoadHandholds` is called — that's inefficient but should still work. The real issue might be that by room 3, either the MaxZones limit of 8000 is being hit, or the deduplication HashSet is marking room 3's cells as already processed from earlier rooms.

Examining the CellKey function...

The region is derived from the root's instance ID, so different room roots should get different regions. But there's a potential hash collision between the `RegionKey` used for explore scans and the `region` used in `FillRoot` — they use different bit shifts and don't include the same coordinates, so they could theoretically collide. Since White Knuckle is a vertical climbing game with rooms stacked upward, the Y coordinate changes significantly between rooms, and CellKey includes Y, so cells should differ. But if two rooms happen to hash to the same region, their cells might be incorrectly deduplicated.

I'm thinking the cleanest fix is to drop the region-based salting entirely and rely on absolute world positions for deduplication, since cells are already keyed by coordinates. Then I need to redesign the system to be more robust: make zone detection bulletproof with larger trigger boxes and continuous scanning around the player, not just on room load; use world-position-only cell keys; scan per-FixedUpdate or per-Update; increase the MaxZones cap; verify the Harmony hook is capturing arguments correctly; and add a manual debug trigger (like F8) to force a full scan and dump diagnostics.

Writing detection fix... Refining the collision detection logic...

Found both bugs:

1. **Kill bug**: `UT_DamagePlayer.DamagePlayer` is an **instance method** on a MonoBehaviour, not static. My call passed `null` as the instance and it silently failed inside the try/catch. The real kill method is `ENT_Player.Kill(string)` (inherited from `GameEntity`).

2. **Texture bug**: I was looking for a `Renderer` on the *collider's* GameObject. In most Unity level setups, mesh colliders sit on a different GameObject than the visible renderer. My sampler never found one, so every spike fell back to the dark-metal material (~6% gray) which reads as black in the game's lighting.

Fixing both, plus dumping exceptions so future bugs surface in the log:

 I'm seeing the core issue now — `UT_DamagePlayer.DamagePlayer` is an instance method, not static, so calling `Invoke(null, args)` silently fails. The method chain goes through `ENT_Player.GetPlayer`, creates damage info, and calls `GameEntity.Damage`, while the kill methods exist on both the player and entity classes.

The simplest path forward is to call `GameEntity.Kill(string)` directly on the player, since that's the base class method that handles death. Alternatively, I could use `Object.FindObjectOfType<UT_DamagePlayer>()` to get the singleton instance if I really need the damage flow, but the kill method is more direct.

For the texture issue, instead of creating a new Standard material from scratch, I should clone the level's existing material directly—that preserves whatever shader and textures the game is already using, likely URP. I'll iterate through the level colliders to find one with an actual texture on its renderer, then use that as the source for cloning. This way the spike cones will match the floor's appearance exactly.

The user said the spikes are still black, which suggests either the fallback is being used (no texture found) or we're hitting a different code path entirely. The real problem is probably that the colliders we're iterating don't have renderers attached—in Unity, the mesh collider and the renderer are often on different GameObjects. So I need to flip my approach: instead of walking colliders looking for renderers, I should walk all renderers in the scene to find one with a valid texture, then sample from that.

Writing texture sampling logic... The material cloning approach preserves shader properties while letting me tint the color, so that should work better. I'm realizing the fallback color might be too dark—that near-black gray could easily read as completely black in the game's lighting. The shader lookups probably aren't failing entirely since the user sees *something* rendered, but I should brighten the fallback color and make sure the texture sampling runs first so we rely on it less.

For the kill method, I need to use reflection to call `Kill(String)` directly on the player instance instead of trying to invoke the static damage method, which was throwing a TargetException. I'm checking whether to use the single-parameter version from the base class or the two-parameter overload on `ENT_Player`.

Writing the reflection wrapper...

Deployed. **TheFloorIsLava.dll v1.3.3** with several layered fixes that all attack the "totally black" symptom from different angles:

### Why the cones were black

`URP/Lit` was rendering them, but in a dim cave with no nearby light source the `albedo × lighting` term collapsed to (near) zero, so the texture-bound cone came out black. On top of that my level-renderer walk was happy to pick up tiny icon textures, UI sprites, or any other texture the floor renderers happened to reference — none of which look like level art.

### Fixes in v1.3.3

**1. Unlit-first shader chain.** Unlit shaders write `texture × tint` straight to the framebuffer with no lighting math, so they can't render black due to dim scenes. The new candidate order:

```127:143:c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle Mod\SpikeBalanceMod\Spikes\SpikeField.cs
    private static Shader? FindUsableShader(out string name)
    {
        string[] candidates =
        {
            "Universal Render Pipeline/Unlit",
            "Unlit/Texture",
            "Universal Render Pipeline/Simple Lit",
            "Universal Render Pipeline/Lit",
            "Standard",
            "Legacy Shaders/Diffuse",
            "Sprites/Default",
        };
```

**2. Bright tint + emission belt-and-suspenders.** The texture is bound through `_BaseMap`, `_MainTex`, `_BaseColorMap`, and `_EmissionMap`; `_EMISSION` keyword is enabled and `_EmissionColor` is set so even if we somehow end up on a lit shader path the cones self-illuminate.

**3. Texture quality filter.** Skips icons / UI / fonts / decals and anything under 128px — those gave us small, mostly transparent or all-color textures that read as black.

```176:189:c:\Users\TFS\Documents\projects\White Knuckle Mod\SpikeBalanceMod\Spikes\SpikeField.cs
    private static bool IsLikelyUiTexture(string texName, string rendName, string shaderName)
    {
        bool BadName(string n) =>
            n.IndexOf("icon", System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 ||
            n.IndexOf("ui", System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 ||
            n.IndexOf("hud", System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 ||
            n.IndexOf("sprite", System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 ||
            n.IndexOf("font", System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 ||
            n.IndexOf("decal", System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0;
        return BadName(texName) || BadName(rendName) ||
               shaderName.IndexOf("UI", System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 ||
               shaderName.IndexOf("Sprite", System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0;
    }
```

**4. Sane fallback.** If no level texture is found yet, we build a `BuildPlainSpikeMaterial` using the same unlit chain — solid bright-stone color, also emissive. No more "Shader.Find(Standard) returned null → render black".

**5. Diagnostic logging.** Every material build now prints either:

```
Spike material built: shader='Universal Render Pipeline/Unlit' tex='WallStone_AlbedoTransparency' (1024x1024) source='Universal Render Pipeline/Lit' from 'WallSegment_03' (inspected=412, withMat=412, withTex=287, goodTex=18).
```

or:

```
No suitable level texture found yet (inspected=…, withMat=…, withTex=…, goodTex=…). Falling back to bright-metal material.
Plain spike material built: shader='…' (no level texture yet).
```

So next launch we'll be able to read in `LogOutput.log` exactly which shader was selected and which texture was bound — if the cones are *still* off after this, please paste the line that begins with `Spike material built:` (or `Plain spike material built:`) and the surrounding 3-4 lines. That tells us whether the issue is shader selection, texture rejection, or something else.
